name: restructure
description: Monorepo restructure: contract/, cluster/kind/, platform/. Establishes clean separation between cluster provider and platform. Archives all non-kind providers. First increment of the new architecture.
sprintGoal: contract/v1/ is validated, cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh and platform/deploy.sh exist and pass shellcheck — the three-layer architecture skeleton is in place and lint-clean.

stories.title: Contract layer: cluster.schema.yaml, validate.py, test fixtures
stories.description: Create contract/v1/cluster.schema.yaml defining the sovereign cluster contract invariants. Create contract/validate.py that validates a cluster config YAML against the schema and exits 1 with an 'AUTARKY VIOLATION' message on constraint failures. Create test fixtures: contract/v1/tests/valid.yaml (passes validation) and contract/v1/tests/invalid-egress-not-blocked.yaml (fails with AUTARKY VIOLATION).
acceptanceCriteria: contract/v1/cluster.schema.yaml exists and is valid YAML: python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('contract/v1/cluster.schema.yaml'))" exits 0
python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/valid.yaml exits 0 with output containing 'CONTRACT VALID'
python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/invalid-egress-not-blocked.yaml exits 1 with output containing 'AUTARKY VIOLATION'
stories.testPlan: python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('contract/v1/cluster.schema.yaml'))" && echo 'schema OK'; python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/valid.yaml; python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/invalid-egress-not-blocked.yaml; show all output verbatim.

smart.notes: Specific score 3: the description says 'defining the sovereign cluster contract invariants' but does not enumerate which invariants must be present in cluster.schema.yaml. The invalid fixture is named 'invalid-egress-not-blocked.yaml' implying an egress-blocking rule, but no authoritative list of required constraints is given — Ralph must infer them. Add a bullet list of required schema properties (e.g. egressBlocked: true, imageRegistryPolicy, etc.) to remove ambiguity.

stories.title: Cluster skeleton: cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh and platform/deploy.sh
stories.description: Create cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh (kind cluster provisioner script) and platform/deploy.sh (platform deployment orchestrator). Both scripts must pass shellcheck -S error. Confirm bootstrap/providers/ is removed from the working tree (archived in git history — [ ! -d bootstrap/providers ] passes).
acceptanceCriteria: cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh exists and shellcheck -S error cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh exits 0
platform/deploy.sh exists and shellcheck -S error platform/deploy.sh exits 0
bootstrap/providers/ does not exist in working tree: [ ! -d bootstrap/providers ] exits 0
stories.testPlan: shellcheck -S error cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh; shellcheck -S error platform/deploy.sh; [ ! -d bootstrap/providers ] && echo 'providers archived OK'; show all output verbatim.

smart.notes: Specific score 4: both scripts are named and their shellcheck gate is clear, but no minimum content spec is given for cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh (e.g. must create a kind cluster named 'sovereign-test', must accept --config argument). A skeleton that passes shellcheck but does nothing would technically satisfy the AC. Consider adding one behavioral criterion such as 'bootstrap.sh --help exits 0 and mentions kind'.

stories.title: Chart migration: platform/charts/ and cluster/kind/charts/ explicit placement
stories.description: Migrate (and verify) all chart directories to the correct tier. Platform-layer charts (_globals, argocd, backstage, chaos-mesh, code-server, crossplane, falco, gitlab, harbor, istio, k6-operator, keycloak, kiali, loki, mailhog, opa-gatekeeper, prometheus-stack, reportportal, selenium-grid, sonarqube, sovereign-pm, tempo, thanos, trivy-operator, wiremock) must be under platform/charts/. Cluster-layer charts (cert-manager, cilium, sealed-secrets) must be under cluster/kind/charts/. The root charts/ directory must be empty to confirm no charts remain in the old location.
acceptanceCriteria: ls platform/charts/ contains all 25 platform charts: _globals argocd backstage chaos-mesh code-server crossplane falco gitlab harbor istio k6-operator keycloak kiali loki mailhog opa-gatekeeper prometheus-stack reportportal selenium-grid sonarqube sovereign-pm tempo thanos trivy-operator wiremock
ls cluster/kind/charts/ contains all 3 cluster charts: cert-manager cilium sealed-secrets
root charts/ is empty: [ -z "$(ls charts/ 2>/dev/null)" ] exits 0
stories.testPlan: ls platform/charts/; ls cluster/kind/charts/; [ -z "$(ls charts/ 2>/dev/null)" ] && echo 'root charts/ empty'; show all output verbatim.

smart.notes: Specific score 3 (downgraded from 4): the 25+3 chart lists are explicit, but the story omits a critical cross-cutting requirement: ArgoCD app manifests in argocd-apps/ currently reference 'path: charts/<name>' (old root path) and must be updated to 'path: platform/charts/<name>' or 'path: cluster/kind/charts/<name>' — without this, ArgoCD breaks after the migration. This is a missing required task, not a minor gap. Time-bound score 4: the unmentioned ArgoCD manifest updates (20+ files) represent unstated scope. Achievable score 4: moving 28 chart directories is mechanical but high in file-operation volume; the hidden ArgoCD reference updates add more work than the 2-point budget suggests.

stories.title: Kaizen: Fix attempts field — initialize to 0, increment only on review re-open
stories.description: The attempts field is currently initialized to 1 after first implementation, causing advance.py to report 0% first-pass rate for all stories. Fix: initialize to 0 in story schema, increment only when review ceremony re-opens a story (adds a reviewNotes entry). Update the formula in advance.py to use len(reviewNotes) == 0 as the first-pass check.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ralph/lib/advance.py uses len(story.get('reviewNotes', [])) == 0 for first-pass rate calculation
shellcheck or python3 -m py_compile scripts/ralph/lib/advance.py exits 0
stories.testPlan: Show schema default and advance.py formula change. Run py_compile. Show output verbatim.

smart.notes: Relevant score 4: this is a process kaizen (attempts-field accuracy), not directly required by the restructure sprint goal (contract/v1/ + cluster/kind/ + platform/ skeleton). Could be deferred to a dedicated kaizen increment without blocking the restructure work.
